title: OpenArm Wave -1 normalization ledger
description: One executable row per resolved specification contradiction (02a §1.3), plus reference-integrity notes (02a §1.4). Field set is owned by 02a §1.2; where the two disagree, §1.2 is canonical and this file is the defect. The winner field is a NON-EMPTY LIST: §1.2 says '1개', but four of the six §1.3 rulings win a gate pair, an invariant carried by two NFRs, or several D/M entries, so a single value cannot carry them.

winner_id.description: A canonical surviving id. FR/NFR are defined in docs/spec tables, D-n/M-n in docs/spec/16, PG-* in docs/plan/03. Bare PG-RT-001 is rejected: it is not a gate id (06 CI-11b).

section.description: Numbered section addressable in the source document, e.g. '3.5' or '2.10'.

discarded_item.description: One invalidated span. `quote` is verbatim source content (emphasis markers and run-of-whitespace excepted); `req` names the requirement whose reading is discarded, when the span belongs to one.

contract.description: The namespace that owns the ruling. A frozen contract generation (CTR-<NAME>@v<n>), or the gate-registry owner for NORM-003, whose ruling lives on the gate axis rather than a contract axis (02a §1.3).

enforcement_item.description: One executable check that enforces the ruling. `check` resolves to an existing CI executable (status active) or to the issued work package that implements it (status planned); a name that resolves to neither is name-only and rejected (02a §1.5 WP-N1-02 acceptance ③).

note.description: A reference-integrity finding that is not a contradiction (02a §1.4). It carries no winner or contract; it records a dangling citation and its correction.
